The compiler sees the graph or it sees nothing.
No pointers. No alibis. Full visibility.
Constrain the language. Expand the optimizer.
Give up escape hatches. Gain whole-program control.
A severe machine for explicit programs.
One codebase, one analysis graph, no split semantics.
Refuse local chaos; keep global certainty.
Vexel optimizes what it can prove, not what it can guess.
